user_message: Working on Contoso Ltd's migration architecture -- need you to prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 13:49 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 17:31 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Contoso Ltd migration architecture

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 08:08 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-03-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Contoso Ltd)

[00:01:30] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into migration architecture.

[00:05:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:17:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-H trap (Skips 'open items from last meeting'); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-H failure mode (Skips 'open items from last meeting')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-H -- Skips 'open items from last meeting'
